title: fagraea berteroana var. sair
intro: Fagraea berteroana var. sair, also known as the Sair Tree or Fagraea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae, native to the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in Polynesian medicine, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves and bark, which are said to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Fagraea berteroana var. sair include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in wound healing and skin conditions. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often made into a tea or infused oil, which can be used topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
